title: Ecosystem
text: The goal is a modular family of packages rather than one package that does everything. Each package should have a narrow scientific responsibility and a clear data handoff.

title: Ecosystem
text:
Package roles


sagui
Photometric segmentation of broadband or medium-band image cubes. The main science product is a segmentation map and flux-conserving regional photometry.


saguiSED
SED fitting for the regional photometry exported by sagui. It handles filter sets, optional backend calls, SED-fit plots, physical-property maps, and post-fit map regularization.


capivara
Spectral segmentation and IFU-oriented workflows. Spectral fitting belongs in capivara or in a capivara-specific companion package, not in saguiSED.

title: Ecosystem
text:
Why separate packages?
Keeping SED fitting outside sagui avoids forcing users to install heavy Python dependencies when they only need segmentation and regional photometry. It also makes it easier to add or replace SED-fitting backends without changing the core segmentation code.
The same philosophy should hold for future packages:

segmentation packages define regions and extract fluxes
fitting packages infer physical quantities from those fluxes
visualization packages can build paper figures from saved products
survey-specific packages can encode filter sets and data-release conventions

title: Ecosystem
text:
Data handoff
The handoff between packages should be boring and explicit: CSV tables for regional photometry and FITS files for image-plane maps.
sed <- as_sagui_sed_table(
  "region_seds_wide.csv",
  filter_set = lsst_filter_set(),
  unit = "nJy",
  redshift = 0.2
)
This keeps downstream SED fitting reproducible even when the segmentation package changes.

title: Ecosystem
text:
Regularization layer
Graph-Laplacian smoothing is implemented as a post-fit map estimator in saguiSED, because it acts on region-level physical properties rather than on the original image cube. The independent backend summaries remain available and should be retained for tables and uncertainty checks.
maps_smooth <- smooth_sed_property_maps(
  labels = seg$cluster_map,
  fit = fit,
  lambda = 3,
  adjacency = "queen"
)
For the paper, this distinction matters: the SED fitting is independent by region; the smoothed maps are spatially regularized summaries of those independent fits.

title: Backends
text: saguiSED is a lightweight R interface around optional SED-fitting backends. The package prepares regional photometry, writes backend inputs, collects fit summaries, and paints fitted quantities back onto the segmentation map.

title: Backends
text:
Bagpipes
The current backend is Bagpipes. Bagpipes is developed independently of saguiSED; this package only provides a small bridge from region-integrated photometric SED tables to the Bagpipes Python interface.
Install and cite Bagpipes following the official Bagpipes documentation.
check_bagpipes()

title: Backends
text:
Model helper
bagpipes_model() stores the compact set of choices currently exposed by saguiSED.
model <- bagpipes_model(
  sfh = "exponential",
  dust = "calzetti",
  metallicity = "free",
  systematic_frac = 0.10
)
The helper is intentionally simple. For full Bagpipes model flexibility, use Bagpipes directly and pass saguiSED regional photometry as input.

title: Backends
text:
Fitting
fit <- fit_region_seds(
  sed,
  backend = "bagpipes",
  model = model,
  redshift = 1.10,
  out_dir = "bagpipes_region_fits"
)
The backend writes a self-contained output folder containing:

regional_photometry_input.csv
filter_set.csv
region_fit_summary.csv
model_photometry.csv
model_spectrum.csv, when returned by the backend
per-region diagnostic plots generated by the backend

saguiSED then reads these files into a sagui_sed_fit object.

title: Backends
text:
What belongs here
saguiSED is for SED fitting of region-integrated photometric measurements. It does not implement IFU spectral fitting. Spectroscopy-focused fitting should live in capivara or a capivara-specific extension, where the data model and scientific assumptions are different.

title: Backends
text:
Backend roadmap
The backend layer is meant to be replaceable. Bagpipes is the first backend because it is widely used and naturally supports photometric SED fitting. Future backends can follow the same pattern:

read a sagui_sed_table
write backend-specific inputs
return a sagui_sed_fit object
expose comparable summary columns for map painting

This keeps the core sagui segmentation package small and lets SED-fitting dependencies evolve independently.

title: Workflows
text: This page shows how saguiSED is used after the segmentation step. The input is a regional photometry table: one row per region and one flux/error pair per filter.

title: Workflows
text:
Common setup
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(sagui)
  library(saguiSED)
  library(ggplot2)
})

sagui_palette <- c("#213E60", "#94B6EF", "#F4F2EF", "#E68C3A")

title: Workflows
text:
From sagui to saguiSED
Run the segmentation in sagui, then extract flux-conserving regional SEDs.




Sagui-10 segmentation used as a representative regional support map.



seg <- segment_regions(
  input = cube,
  Ncomp = 20,
  use_starlet_mask = TRUE,
  starlet_J = 5,
  starlet_scales = 2:5
)

regional_seds <- extract_region_sed(
  cube = cube,
  labels = seg$cluster_map,
  band_values = band_wavelengths,
  error_fallback = "mad_sky"
)
The summed regional flux is the SED-fitting input. Mean SEDs can be useful as diagnostics, but the summed table preserves total flux.
utils::write.csv(
  regional_seds$flux_wide,
  "region_seds_wide.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)

title: Workflows
text:
Filter sets
For JWST/NIRCam photometry, use the NIRCam response curves bundled with sagui.
filters <- jwst_nircam_filter_set()
For Rubin/LSST-style photometry, use the response curves bundled with saguiSED.
filters <- lsst_filter_set()
Custom filter curves are accepted through sed_filter_set().
filters <- sed_filter_set(
  throughput = "my_filter_curves.csv",
  filters = c("F090W", "F115W", "F150W", "F200W"),
  name = "custom",
  wavelength_unit = "micron"
)
The throughput table must contain a filter name, wavelength, and throughput column. Additional effective-wavelength columns can be supplied when available.

title: Workflows
text:
Fitting regional SEDs
Convert the wide table and call the backend.
sed <- as_sagui_sed_table(
  "region_seds_wide.csv",
  filter_set = filters,
  unit = "10nJy",
  redshift = 1.10,
  n_pix_col = "n_pix"
)

fit <- fit_region_seds(
  sed,
  backend = "bagpipes",
  model = bagpipes_model(
    sfh = "exponential",
    dust = "calzetti",
    metallicity = "free",
    systematic_frac = 0.10
  ),
  out_dir = "bagpipes_region_fits"
)
The fit object contains:
names(fit)

summary: one row per fitted region.
model_photometry: observed and model photometry per filter.
model_spectrum: model spectrum returned by the backend, when available.
filter_set: the response curves used by the backend.

title: Workflows
text:
SED fit panels
plot_sed_fit_mosaic(
  fit,
  normalize = "none",
  ncol = 4,
  transmission_height = 0.2,
  point_size = 2.8,
  base_size = 12
)
Filter points are colored by wavelength. The model line is kept visually separate from the observed photometry so the plot remains readable on the website and in notebooks.




Example Bagpipes SED fit for Sagui-8 region 8 using SAGUI regional photometry.

title: Workflows
text:
Property maps
Paint fitted region summaries back to the image plane:




Example star-formation surface-density map painted back onto a SAGUI segmentation.



maps <- paint_sed_properties(
  seg$cluster_map,
  fit,
  properties = c("logMformed", "logSFR", "logsSFRformed", "age_gyr", "logZ_Zsun", "Av")
)

save_sed_property_map_pngs(
  maps,
  out_dir = "property_maps_png",
  prefix = "example"
)

write_property_maps(
  maps,
  out_dir = "property_maps_fits",
  prefix = "example"
)

write_property_cube(
  maps,
  path = "example_property_cube.fits"
)
The FITS cube is useful for opening several derived quantities in tools such as SAOImageDS9. The CSV manifest records which property is stored in each plane.

title: Workflows
text:
Regularized maps
Independent region fits are the primary result. For map-level interpretation, saguiSED can also smooth fitted quantities on the region-adjacency graph.
maps_smooth <- smooth_sed_property_maps(
  seg$cluster_map,
  fit,
  lambda = 3,
  adjacency = "queen",
  unsmoothed_properties = "fit_rms_sigma"
)

save_sed_property_map_pngs(
  maps_smooth,
  out_dir = "property_maps_smooth_png",
  prefix = "example"
)

write_property_cube(
  maps_smooth,
  path = "example_property_cube_smooth.fits"
)
This is a post-processing estimator. It does not refit the photometry and does not change the independent Bagpipes summaries.




Sagui-11 low-surface-brightness segmentation shown as a more complex support map.

title: Workflows
text:
Units
as_sagui_sed_table() currently supports:

Jy
uJy
nJy
10nJy

Use the unit that matches the regional table. If the image cube is stored in 10 nJy, pass unit = "10nJy" rather than converting manually.

title: saguiSED
text:
SED fitting for segmented photometry
    saguiSED
    
      Fit region-integrated photometric SEDs produced by sagui,
      then paint physical-property summaries back onto the segmentation map.
      Heavy SED-fitting dependencies stay outside the core segmentation package.
    
    
      Quick start
      Workflows
      Backends
      GitHub
    
  
  
    
      
    
    
      
      Part of the COIN Toolbox

title: saguiSED
text:
What saguiSED does
saguiSED is a companion package for the photometric SED-fitting stage of a segmented-galaxy workflow. The core sagui package decides how pixels are grouped and exports flux-conserving regional SEDs. saguiSED starts from those regional SED tables.


1. Read regional photometry
Convert a sagui regional SED table, or a compatible CSV, into a sagui_sed_table.


2. Fit region SEDs
Call an optional backend, currently Bagpipes, while keeping Python dependencies outside the core segmentation package.


3. Paint maps
Paint fitted physical quantities back onto the segmentation map and optionally regularize them on the region-adjacency graph.

title: saguiSED
text:
Showcase
The figures below show the intended output style: a regional SED fit, a segmentation map used as spatial support, and a fitted quantity painted back to the image plane.





Bagpipes SED fit for Sagui-8 region 8 using flux-conserving SAGUI regional photometry.


Regional SED fit





Sagui-10 segmentation map used as the spatial template for downstream regional SED fitting.


Segmentation support





Example physical-property map painted onto the Sagui-10 segmentation.


Painted property map

title: saguiSED
text:
Installation
install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("RafaelSdeSouza/saguiSED")
library(saguiSED)
The Bagpipes backend is optional and runs through Python. Install Bagpipes following the official Bagpipes documentation.
check_bagpipes()

title: saguiSED
text:
Quick start
Start from a flux-conserving regional SED table exported by sagui.
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(sagui)
  library(saguiSED)
})

filters <- jwst_nircam_filter_set()

sed <- as_sagui_sed_table(
  "sagui10_region_seds_wide.csv",
  filter_set = filters,
  unit = "10nJy",
  redshift = 1.10,
  n_pix_col = "n_pix"
)
Fit all regions with the Bagpipes backend:
fit <- fit_region_seds(
  sed,
  backend = "bagpipes",
  model = bagpipes_model(
    sfh = "exponential",
    dust = "calzetti",
    metallicity = "free",
    systematic_frac = 0.10
  ),
  out_dir = "sagui10_bagpipes"
)
Plot the regional SED fits:
plot_sed_fit_mosaic(
  fit,
  normalize = "none",
  transmission_height = 0.2,
  point_size = 2.8
)
Paint fitted quantities back onto the segmentation:
maps <- paint_sed_properties(seg$cluster_map, fit)

save_sed_property_map_pngs(
  maps,
  out_dir = "property_maps_png",
  prefix = "sagui10"
)

write_property_cube(
  maps,
  path = "sagui10_property_cube.fits"
)
Optional graph-Laplacian smoothing is available as a post-processing step:
maps_smooth <- smooth_sed_property_maps(
  seg$cluster_map,
  fit,
  lambda = 3,
  adjacency = "queen"
)

title: saguiSED
text:
Scope
saguiSED is intentionally narrow. It is not the segmentation package and it is not a spectral-fitting package for IFU spectra. The spectroscopy-oriented pieces belong in capivara or capivara-specific extensions. saguiSED focuses on photometric regional SED fitting and map products.

title: saguiSED
text:
Backend credit
The current backend uses Bagpipes for SED fitting. If you use the Bagpipes backend in a scientific analysis, cite Bagpipes according to the guidance in the official Bagpipes documentation, in addition to citing saguiSED and the segmentation package that produced the regional photometry. See References for the citation list.

title: References
text: If you use saguiSED, cite the SED-fitting backend you used, the segmentation package that produced the regional photometry, and the relevant method papers.

title: References
text:
Method papers

sagui
de Souza, R. S. et al. (2026). SAGUI: SED-based Segmentation of Multi-band Galaxy Images – Application to JADES in GOODS-South. arXiv:2604.18812. https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2604.18812


capivara
de Souza, R. S. et al. (2025). capivara: a spectral-based segmentation method for IFU data cubes. Monthly Notices of the Royal Astronomical Society, 539(4), 3166–3179. https://doi.org/10.1093/mnras/staf688


Bagpipes
Carnall, A. C., McLure, R. J., Dunlop, J. S., & Davé, R. (2018). Inferring the star formation histories of massive quiescent galaxies with BAGPIPES: evidence for multiple quenching mechanisms. Monthly Notices of the Royal Astronomical Society, 480(4), 4379–4401. https://doi.org/10.1093/mnras/sty2169
For installation and backend-specific citation guidance, see the Bagpipes documentation.

title: References
text:
Suggested BibTeX
@misc{sagui2026,
  author = {de Souza, Rafael S. and others},
  title = {{SAGUI}: {SED}-based Segmentation of Multi-band Galaxy Images --
           Application to {JADES} in {GOODS}-South},
  year = {2026},
  eprint = {2604.18812},
  archivePrefix = {arXiv},
  primaryClass = {astro-ph.IM},
  doi = {10.48550/arXiv.2604.18812}
}

@article{capivara2025,
  author = {de Souza, Rafael S. and others},
  title = {capivara: a spectral-based segmentation method for {IFU} data cubes},
  journal = {Monthly Notices of the Royal Astronomical Society},
  volume = {539},
  number = {4},
  pages = {3166--3179},
  year = {2025},
  doi = {10.1093/mnras/staf688}
}

@article{bagpipes2018,
  author = {Carnall, A. C. and McLure, R. J. and Dunlop, J. S. and Dav{\'e}, R.},
  title = {Inferring the star formation histories of massive quiescent galaxies
           with {BAGPIPES}: evidence for multiple quenching mechanisms},
  journal = {Monthly Notices of the Royal Astronomical Society},
  volume = {480},
  number = {4},
  pages = {4379--4401},
  year = {2018},
  doi = {10.1093/mnras/sty2169}
}
When saguiSED receives a software DOI, cite that DOI in addition to the method and backend papers above.
